verses.latin_vulgate: Confortamini, filii Benjamin, in medio Jerusalem :  et in Thecua clangite buccina,  et super Bethacarem levate vexillum,  quia malum visum est ab aquilone,  et contritio magna.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָעִ֣זוּ ׀ בְּנֵ֣י בִנְיָמִ֗ן מִקֶּ֙רֶב֙ יְר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם וּבִתְק֙וֹעַ֙ תִּקְע֣וּ שׁוֹפָ֔ר וְעַל־בֵּ֥ית הַכֶּ֖רֶם שְׂא֣וּ מַשְׂאֵ֑ת כִּ֥י רָעָ֛ה נִשְׁקְפָ֥ה מִצָּפ֖וֹן וְשֶׁ֥בֶר גָּדֽוֹל׃
verses.king_james: O ye children of Benjamin, gather yourselves to flee out of the midst of Jerusalem, and blow the trumpet in Tekoa, and set up a sign of fire in Beth–haccerem: for evil appeareth out of the north, and great destruction.

verses.latin_vulgate: Speciosæ et delicatæ assimilavi filiam Sion.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַנָּוָה֙ וְהַמְּעֻנָּגָ֔ה דָּמִ֖יתִי בַּת־צִיּֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: I have likened the daughter of Zion to a comely and delicate woman.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad eam venient pastores et greges eorum ;  fixerunt in ea tentoria in circuitu :  pascet unusquisque eos qui sub manu sua sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵלֶ֛יהָ יָבֹ֥אוּ רֹעִ֖ים וְעֶדְרֵיהֶ֑ם תָּקְע֨וּ עָלֶ֤יהָ אֹֽהָלִים֙ סָבִ֔יב רָע֖וּ אִ֥ישׁ אֶת־יָדֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The shepherds with their flocks shall come unto her; they shall pitch their tents against her round about; they shall feed every one in his place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sanctificate super eam bellum :  consurgite, et ascendamus in meridie :  væ nobis, quia declinavit dies ;  quia longiores factæ sunt umbræ vesperi !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קַדְּשׁ֤וּ עָלֶ֙יהָ֙ מִלְחָמָ֔ה ק֖וּמוּ וְנַעֲלֶ֣ה בַֽצׇּהֳרָ֑יִם א֥וֹי לָ֙נוּ֙ כִּֽי־פָנָ֣ה הַיּ֔וֹם כִּ֥י יִנָּט֖וּ צִלְלֵי־עָֽרֶב׃
verses.king_james: Prepare ye war against her; arise, and let us go up at noon. Woe unto us! for the day goeth away, for the shadows of the evening are stretched out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surgite, et ascendamus in nocte,  et dissipemus domus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֚וּמוּ וְנַעֲלֶ֣ה בַלָּ֔יְלָה וְנַשְׁחִ֖יתָה אַרְמְנוֹתֶֽיהָ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Arise, and let us go by night, and let us destroy her palaces.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia hæc dicit Dominus exercituum :  Cædite lignum ejus,  et fundite circa Jerusalem aggerem.  Hæc est civitas visitationis :  omnis calumnia in medio ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י כֹ֤ה אָמַר֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת כִּרְת֣וּ עֵצָ֔הֿ וְשִׁפְכ֥וּ עַל־יְרוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם סֹֽלְלָ֑ה הִ֚יא הָעִ֣יר הׇפְקַ֔ד כֻּלָּ֖הּ עֹ֥שֶׁק בְּקִרְבָּֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: For thus hath the Lord of hosts said, Hew ye down trees, and cast a mount against Jerusalem: this is the city to be visited; she is wholly oppression in the midst of her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut frigidam fecit cisterna aquam suam,  sic frigidam fecit malitiam suam.  Iniquitas et vastitas audietur in ea,  coram me semper infirmitas et plaga.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּהָקִ֥יר (בור) [בַּ֙יִר֙] מֵימֶ֔יהָ כֵּ֖ן הֵקֵ֣רָה רָעָתָ֑הּ חָמָ֣ס וָ֠שֹׁ֠ד יִשָּׁ֨מַע בָּ֧הּ עַל־פָּנַ֛י תָּמִ֖יד חֳלִ֥י וּמַכָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: As a fountain casteth out her waters, so she casteth out her wickedness: violence and spoil is heard in her; before me continually is grief and wounds.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erudire, Jerusalem,  ne forte recedat anima mea a te ;  ne forte ponam te desertam,  terram inhabitabilem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִוָּֽסְרִי֙ יְר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם פֶּן־תֵּקַ֥ע נַפְשִׁ֖י מִמֵּ֑ךְ פֶּן־אֲשִׂימֵ֣ךְ שְׁמָמָ֔ה אֶ֖רֶץ ל֥וֹא נוֹשָֽׁבָה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Be thou instructed, O Jerusalem, lest my soul depart from thee; lest I make thee desolate, a land not inhabited.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum :  Usque ad racemum colligent quasi in vinea reliquias Israël.  Converte manum tuam quasi vindemiator ad cartallum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֤ה אָמַר֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת עוֹלֵ֛ל יְעוֹלְל֥וּ כַגֶּ֖פֶן שְׁאֵרִ֣ית יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל הָשֵׁב֙ יָֽדְךָ֔ כְּבוֹצֵ֖ר עַל־סַלְסִלּֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord of hosts, They shall throughly glean the remnant of Israel as a vine: turn back thine hand as a grapegatherer into the baskets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui loquar, et quem contestabor ut audiat ?  ecce incircumcisæ aures eorum,  et audire non possunt :  ecce verbum Domini factum est eis in opprobrium,  et non suscipient illud.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־מִ֨י אֲדַבְּרָ֤ה וְאָעִ֙ידָה֙ וְיִשְׁמָ֔עוּ הִנֵּה֙ עֲרֵלָ֣ה אׇזְנָ֔ם וְלֹ֥א יוּכְל֖וּ לְהַקְשִׁ֑יב הִנֵּ֣ה דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֗ה הָיָ֥ה לָהֶ֛ם לְחֶרְפָּ֖ה לֹ֥א יַחְפְּצוּ־בֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: To whom shall I speak, and give warning, that they may hear? behold, their ear is uncircumcised, and they cannot hearken: behold, the word of the Lord is unto them a reproach; they have no delight in it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Idcirco furore Domini plenus sum ;  laboravi sustinens.  Effunde super parvulum foris,  et super consilium juvenum simul :  vir enim cum muliere capietur ;  senex cum pleno dierum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֵת֩ חֲמַ֨ת יְהֹוָ֤ה ׀ מָלֵ֙אתִי֙ נִלְאֵ֣יתִי הָכִ֔יל שְׁפֹ֤ךְ עַל־עוֹלָל֙ בַּח֔וּץ וְעַ֛ל ס֥וֹד בַּחוּרִ֖ים יַחְדָּ֑ו כִּֽי־גַם־אִ֤ישׁ עִם־אִשָּׁה֙ יִלָּכֵ֔דוּ זָקֵ֖ן עִם־מְלֵ֥א יָמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Therefore I am full of the fury of the Lord; I am weary with holding in: I will pour it out upon the children abroad, and upon the assembly of young men together: for even the husband with the wife shall be taken, the aged with him that is full of days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et transibunt domus eorum ad alteros,  agri et uxores pariter,  quia extendam manum meam super habitantes terram, dicit Dominus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנָסַ֤בּוּ בָֽתֵּיהֶם֙ לַאֲחֵרִ֔ים שָׂד֥וֹת וְנָשִׁ֖ים יַחְדָּ֑ו כִּֽי־אַטֶּ֧ה אֶת־יָדִ֛י עַל־יֹשְׁבֵ֥י הָאָ֖רֶץ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: And their houses shall be turned unto others, with their fields and wives together: for I will stretch out my hand upon the inhabitants of the land, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: a minore quippe usque ad majorem  omnes avaritiæ student,  et a propheta usque ad sacerdotem  cuncti faciunt dolum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י מִקְּטַנָּם֙ וְעַד־גְּדוֹלָ֔ם כֻּלּ֖וֹ בּוֹצֵ֣עַ בָּ֑צַע וּמִנָּבִיא֙ וְעַד־כֹּהֵ֔ן כֻּלּ֖וֹ עֹ֥שֶׂה שָּֽׁקֶר׃
verses.king_james: For from the least of them even unto the greatest of them every one is given to covetousness; and from the prophet even unto the priest every one dealeth falsely.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et curabant contritionem filiæ populi mei cum ignominia,  dicentes : Pax, pax !  et non erat pax.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְרַפְּא֞וּ אֶת־שֶׁ֤בֶר עַמִּי֙ עַל־נְקַלָּ֔ה לֵאמֹ֖ר שָׁל֣וֹם ׀ שָׁל֑וֹם וְאֵ֖ין שָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.king_james: They have healed also the hurt of the daughter of my people slightly, saying, Peace, peace; when there is no peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confusi sunt, quia abominationem fecerunt :  quin potius confusione non sunt confusi,  et erubescere nescierunt.  Quam ob rem cadent inter ruentes :  in tempore visitationis suæ corruent, dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֹבִ֕ישׁוּ כִּ֥י תוֹעֵבָ֖ה עָשׂ֑וּ גַּם־בּ֣וֹשׁ לֹֽא־יֵב֗וֹשׁוּ גַּם־הַכְלִים֙ לֹ֣א יָדָ֔עוּ לָכֵ֞ן יִפְּל֧וּ בַנֹּפְלִ֛ים בְּעֵת־פְּקַדְתִּ֥ים יִכָּשְׁל֖וּ אָמַ֥ר יְהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Were they ashamed when they had committed abomination? nay, they were not at all ashamed, neither could they blush: therefore they shall fall among them that fall: at the time that I visit them they shall be cast down, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus :  State super vias, et videte,  et interrogate de semitis antiquis quæ sit via bona,  et ambulate in ea :  et invenietis refrigerium animabus vestris.  Et dixerunt : Non ambulabimus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֣ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֡ה עִמְדוּ֩ עַל־דְּרָכִ֨ים וּרְא֜וּ וְשַׁאֲל֣וּ ׀ לִנְתִב֣וֹת עוֹלָ֗ם אֵי־זֶ֨ה דֶ֤רֶךְ הַטּוֹב֙ וּלְכוּ־בָ֔הּ וּמִצְא֥וּ מַרְגּ֖וֹעַ לְנַפְשְׁכֶ֑ם וַיֹּאמְר֖וּ לֹ֥א נֵלֵֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord, Stand ye in the ways, and see, and ask for the old paths, where is the good way, and walk therein, and ye shall find rest for your souls. But they said, We will not walk therein.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et constitui super vos speculatores :  Audite vocem tubæ.  Et dixerunt : Non audiemus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַהֲקִמֹתִ֤י עֲלֵיכֶם֙ צֹפִ֔ים הַקְשִׁ֖יבוּ לְק֣וֹל שׁוֹפָ֑ר וַיֹּאמְר֖וּ לֹ֥א נַקְשִֽׁיב׃
verses.king_james: Also I set watchmen over you, saying, Hearken to the sound of the trumpet. But they said, We will not hearken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo audite, gentes,  et cognosce, congregatio,  quanta ego faciam eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֖ן שִׁמְע֣וּ הַגּוֹיִ֑ם וּדְעִ֥י עֵדָ֖ה אֶת־אֲשֶׁר־בָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Therefore hear, ye nations, and know, O congregation, what is among them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audi, terra :  ecce ego adducam mala super populum istum,  fructum cogitationum ejus :  quia verba mea non audierunt,  et legem meam projecerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְעִ֣י הָאָ֔רֶץ הִנֵּ֨ה אָנֹכִ֜י מֵבִ֥יא רָעָ֛ה אֶל־הָעָ֥ם הַזֶּ֖ה פְּרִ֣י מַחְשְׁבוֹתָ֑ם כִּ֤י עַל־דְּבָרַי֙ לֹ֣א הִקְשִׁ֔יבוּ וְתוֹרָתִ֖י וַיִּמְאֲסוּ־בָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Hear, O earth: behold, I will bring evil upon this people, even the fruit of their thoughts, because they have not hearkened unto my words, nor to my law, but rejected it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut quid mihi thus de Saba affertis,  et calamum suave olentem de terra longinqua ?  Holocautomata vestra non sunt accepta,  et victimæ vestræ non placuerunt mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָמָּה־זֶּ֨ה לִ֤י לְבוֹנָה֙ מִשְּׁבָ֣א תָב֔וֹא וְקָנֶ֥ה הַטּ֖וֹב מֵאֶ֣רֶץ מֶרְחָ֑ק עֹלֽוֹתֵיכֶם֙ לֹ֣א לְרָצ֔וֹן וְזִבְחֵיכֶ֖ם לֹא־עָ֥רְבוּ לִֽי׃
verses.king_james: To what purpose cometh there to me incense from Sheba, and the sweet cane from a far country? your burnt offerings are not acceptable, nor your sacrifices sweet unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea hæc dicit Dominus :  Ecce ego dabo in populum istum ruinas :  et ruent in eis patres et filii simul ;  vicinus et proximus peribunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֗ן כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה הִנְנִ֥י נֹתֵ֛ן אֶל־הָעָ֥ם הַזֶּ֖ה מִכְשֹׁלִ֑ים וְכָ֣שְׁלוּ בָ֠ם אָב֨וֹת וּבָנִ֥ים יַחְדָּ֛ו שָׁכֵ֥ן וְרֵע֖וֹ (יאבדו) [וְאָבָֽדוּ]׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Therefore thus saith the Lord, Behold, I will lay stumblingblocks before this people, and the fathers and the sons together shall fall upon them; the neighbour and his friend shall perish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus :  Ecce populus venit de terra aquilonis,  et gens magna consurget a finibus terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה הִנֵּ֛ה עַ֥ם בָּ֖א מֵאֶ֣רֶץ צָפ֑וֹן וְג֣וֹי גָּד֔וֹל יֵע֖וֹר מִיַּרְכְּתֵי־אָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord, Behold, a people cometh from the north country, and a great nation shall be raised from the sides of the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sagittam et scutum arripiet :  crudelis est et non miserebitur.  Vox ejus quasi mare sonabit :  et super equos ascendent,  præparati quasi vir ad prælium  adversum te, filia Sion.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קֶ֣שֶׁת וְכִיד֞וֹן יַחֲזִ֗יקוּ אַכְזָרִ֥י הוּא֙ וְלֹ֣א יְרַחֵ֔מוּ קוֹלָם֙ כַּיָּ֣ם יֶהֱמֶ֔ה וְעַל־סוּסִ֖ים יִרְכָּ֑בוּ עָר֗וּךְ כְּאִישׁ֙ לַמִּלְחָמָ֔ה עָלַ֖יִךְ בַּת־צִיּֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: They shall lay hold on bow and spear; they are cruel, and have no mercy; their voice roareth like the sea; and they ride upon horses, set in array as men for war against thee, O daughter of Zion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audivimus famam ejus ;  dissolutæ sunt manus nostræ :  tribulatio apprehendit nos,  dolores ut parturientem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁמַ֥עְנוּ אֶת־שׇׁמְע֖וֹ רָפ֣וּ יָדֵ֑ינוּ צָרָה֙ הֶחֱזִקַ֔תְנוּ חִ֖יל כַּיּֽוֹלֵדָֽה׃
verses.king_james: We have heard the fame thereof: our hands wax feeble: anguish hath taken hold of us, and pain, as of a woman in travail.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite exire ad agros,  et in via ne ambuletis,  quoniam gladius inimici,  pavor in circuitu.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־[תֵּֽצְאוּ֙] (תצאי) הַשָּׂדֶ֔ה וּבַדֶּ֖רֶךְ אַל־[תֵּלֵ֑כוּ] (תלכי) כִּ֚י חֶ֣רֶב לְאֹיֵ֔ב מָג֖וֹר מִסָּבִֽיב׃
verses.king_james: Go not forth into the field, nor walk by the way; for the sword of the enemy and fear is on every side.

verses.latin_vulgate: Filia populi mei, accingere cilicio,  et conspergere cinere :  luctum unigeniti fac tibi,  planctum amarum,  quia repente veniet vastator super nos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּת־עַמִּ֤י חִגְרִי־שָׂק֙ וְהִתְפַּלְּשִׁ֣י בָאֵ֔פֶר אֵ֤בֶל יָחִיד֙ עֲשִׂ֣י לָ֔ךְ מִסְפַּ֖ד תַּמְרוּרִ֑ים כִּ֣י פִתְאֹ֔ם יָבֹ֥א הַשֹּׁדֵ֖ד עָלֵֽינוּ׃
verses.king_james: O daughter of my people, gird thee with sackcloth, and wallow thyself in ashes: make thee mourning, as for an only son, most bitter lamentation: for the spoiler shall suddenly come upon us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Probatorem dedi te in populo meo robustum :  et scies, et probabis viam eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּח֛וֹן נְתַתִּ֥יךָ בְעַמִּ֖י מִבְצָ֑ר וְתֵדַ֕ע וּבָחַנְתָּ֖ אֶת־דַּרְכָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: I have set thee for a tower and a fortress among my people, that thou mayest know and try their way.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes isti principes declinantes,  ambulantes fraudulenter,  æs et ferrum :  universi corrupti sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֻּלָּם֙ סָרֵ֣י סוֹרְרִ֔ים הֹלְכֵ֥י רָכִ֖יל נְחֹ֣שֶׁת וּבַרְזֶ֑ל כֻּלָּ֥ם מַשְׁחִיתִ֖ים הֵֽמָּה׃
verses.king_james: They are all grievous revolters, walking with slanders: they are brass and iron; they are all corrupters.

verses.latin_vulgate: Defecit sufflatorium ;  in igne consumptum est plumbum :  frustra conflavit conflator,  malitiæ enim eorum non sunt consumptæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נָחַ֣ר מַפֻּ֔חַ (מאשתם) [מֵאֵ֖שׁ תַּ֣ם] עֹפָ֑רֶת לַשָּׁוְא֙ צָרַ֣ף צָר֔וֹף וְרָעִ֖ים לֹ֥א נִתָּֽקוּ׃
verses.king_james: The bellows are burned, the lead is consumed of the fire; the founder melteth in vain: for the wicked are not plucked away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Argentum reprobum vocate eos,  quia Dominus projecit illos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֶּ֣סֶף נִמְאָ֔ס קָרְא֖וּ לָהֶ֑ם כִּֽי־מָאַ֥ס יְהֹוָ֖ה בָּהֶֽם׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Reprobate silver shall men call them, because the Lord hath rejected them.
